hard.next_step: Prepare and submit the completed RFI response ahead of the 27-Jul-2026 deadline; RFI issued to 6-7 vendors on 28-Jun-2026.
hard.ais_why: Pinsent Masons is actively exploring AI-led procurement transformation use cases, especially Merlin AI/control tower, guided buyer experience, contract repository, automated contract redlining, compare-to-playbook capability, workflow orchestration, and spend visibility. AI is relevant because their current process has fragmented systems, manual redlining, duplicate data entry, limited orchestration, and poor end-to-end visibility.
hard.competitor: Scanmarket (Unit4) incumbent; do-nothing / status quo; 6-7 unnamed RFI peer vendors; Ligura (contract AI); Halo (ITSM intake)
hard.primary_competitor: Do-nothing / Scanmarket (Unit4) renewal within a wide 6-7 vendor RFI

north_star_verdict.headline: Live, recently engaged RFI phase: 26-Jun demo delivered, RFI clarification answers exchanged 3-Jul, response due 27-Jul. On pace for an 18-month enterprise S2P cycle, but single-threaded on the Head of Procurement with no economic buyer engaged and budget above the existing source-to-contract envelope gated behind an unwritten business case.
north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2027-12-31, ~543 days out; record created 2026-06-12 (24 days old); stage Formal Evaluation entered 2026-06-30. Time-in-stage is short and healthy for an RFI issued 28-Jun with a 27-Jul response deadline (a 29-day window). Last real buyer touch 2026-07-03 (Daniel Simmonds returned RFI clarification answers). No forward slip. Immediate milestone: RFI submission by 27-Jul; then shortlist to 3-4 vendors (~Aug-2026), tailored demos, decision, and a project timed to the next financial year (from 1-May-2027) against the Dec-2027 Scanmarket contract expiry forcing function.
evidence: Stage Formal Evaluation and forecast Pipeline are appropriate for an active RFI phase: RFI officially issued to 6-7 vendors on 28-Jun-2026 with a 27-Jul-2026 response deadline (Next Step; Avoma demo 26-Jun-2026).

Deal is LIVE, not cold: last activity 2026-07-03 when Daniel Simmonds returned the RFI clarification answers ('Completed questions, any queries please let me know. Thanks Dan'), following Zycus's clarification questions sent 1-Jul (SF activity 3-Jul-2026).

45-minute art-of-the-possible demo delivered 26-Jun-2026 covering Merlin intake in Teams, autonomous negotiation (Anna), contract insta-review, spend analytics and supplier risk; Daniel called it 'a million miles away from where we are' and 'really, really good' (Avoma 26-Jun-2026).

No economic buyer engaged: Daniel stated on 12-Jun 'the COO is aware that we're looking at, but very much driven by us', and any spend above the existing budget 'would be a budget book business case' (Avoma 12-Jun-2026).

Single-threaded: Daniel Simmonds (Head of Procurement) is the sole decision-level contact; a procurement data analyst attended the 26-Jun demo but is not a buyer; no finance, IT or C-level contact is engaged.

Budget-constrained scope: Daniel funds only source-to-contract and spend analytics, flagged P2P/invoicing as finance's responsibility and 'who knows' if in scope, and declined to disclose the budget figure (Avoma 26-Jun-2026).
Forcing function: incumbent Scanmarket (Unit4) source-to-contract contract expires Dec-2027, and the project is scoped to the next financial year from 1-May-2027 (Avoma 12-Jun and 26-Jun-2026).

metrics.narrative: Daniel Simmonds quantified the operating context on the 12-Jun discovery call: roughly £120M indirect spend over the last 12 months, a 14-person corporate team (5 procurement, 5 contract lawyers) plus a buying community that has grown from ~30 to ~100 end users across ~3,500-4,000 staff. The pain is concrete but the value case is not yet monetised: fragmented systems (Scanmarket, Halo, Chrome River, Anydata, Ligura, DocuSign, Adderant) cause duplicate data entry, no single source of truth, and no PO functionality. Anthony Gray cited Zycus benchmarks of ~40% procurement time recovered and 3-4% tail savings on the 26-Jun demo, but Daniel did not validate or adopt those numbers. Converting the £120M spend and manual-process pain into a customer-owned savings/ROI figure is the open move and the seed of the required business case.
sources: Avoma discovery call 12-Jun-2026 (Daniel Simmonds: £120M indirect spend, 14-person team, ~100 end users)
Avoma demo 26-Jun-2026 (Anthony Gray: ~40% efficiency, 3-4% tail savings, not validated)

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named or engaged. On the 12-Jun call Daniel Simmonds said 'the COO is aware that we're looking at, but very much driven by us', placing the mandate at procurement level rather than the executive suite. Budget authority is split and undisclosed: Daniel controls an existing budget for source-to-contract and spend analytics, but anything above it 'would be a budget book business case' requiring approval he cannot grant alone, and finance owns the P2P/invoice-approval scope ('very much their responsibility, not ours'). When Claire Hudson asked for the budget threshold on 26-Jun, Daniel declined ('I won't share what our existing budget is with you'). The evidence points to the COO and a finance lead as the real approvers, but neither is named in Salesforce or on a call, so identifying and reaching them is a ranked move.
sources: Avoma discovery call 12-Jun-2026 (Daniel: COO aware, procurement-driven)
Avoma demo 26-Jun-2026 (Daniel: business case for above-budget spend; budget undisclosed; finance owns P2P)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are visible in the RFI structure and Daniel's own words. On 12-Jun he described a spreadsheet of ~60 high-level requirements for an end-to-end pay-to-pay platform, testing 'can you meet that, is that standard configuration', plus a request for indicative pricing at RFI stage. On the 26-Jun demo the buyer-weighted criteria surfaced: guided buyer experience/intake ('a guided buyer experience' is 'ideally where we want to get to'), consolidation of a fragmented stack onto one data core, PO functionality (which they lack today), integration with Adderant (their practice-management ERP) and coexistence with Ligura, and contract AI/insta-review for third-party paper. Catalog-heavy buying is explicitly a weak fit ('we don't do a lot of catalog'). Criteria are confirmed at a high level but not yet weighted or scored, which the tailored post-shortlist demos are meant to resolve.
sources: Avoma discovery call 12-Jun-2026 (~60 requirements, end-to-end P2P, indicative pricing)
Avoma demo 26-Jun-2026 (guided intake, PO functionality, Adderant/Ligura integration, contract AI)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a structured but early formal evaluation, and the call sequence maps the process: 12-Jun discovery (Daniel + Zycus VP Anthony Gray), NDA signed 16-Jun, 26-Jun art-of-the-possible demo, RFI issued to 6-7 vendors 28-Jun with a 27-Jul response deadline. Daniel described the forward path on both calls: evaluate RFI responses, 'shortlist probably 3, 4', run 'more detailed demos with the wider team', then decide whether to go to a full RFP or a direct award, and whether additional budget/business case is needed. The approval chain beyond Daniel is a business case to finance/COO for spend above the existing budget. Daniel was candid that 'what it could look like beyond the RFI, I don't know', so the process is defined only through shortlist; the decision and paper steps are not yet formalised.
sources: Avoma discovery call 12-Jun-2026 (shortlist to 3-4, wider-team demos, possible RFP or direct award)
Avoma demo 26-Jun-2026 (process 'up in the air' beyond RFI)
SF field history StageName Qualified -> Formal Evaluation 30-Jun-2026

paper_process.narrative: Early contracting mechanics are partly visible. An NDA was drafted by Jamie James (Pinsent Masons) and executed via DocuSign on 16-Jun-2026, routed to Anthony Gray (Zycus VP EMEA) as signatory, so a mutual NDA is in place. The commercial forcing function is the incumbent Scanmarket (Unit4) source-to-contract contract expiring Dec-2027, with the project scoped to the buyer's next financial year from 1-May-2027. As a UK deal, a missing PO is normal and not a red flag. No MSA/SOW, procurement channel, InfoSec questionnaire or legal-review path has been discussed yet, which is appropriate for the RFI phase; visibility will come once Zycus is shortlisted and commercials begin. The buyer signs third-party paper 'a mix' of the time, which raises the value of Zycus contract tooling later.
sources: SF activity 16-Jun-2026 (NDA signed via DocuSign; Jamie James issued, Anthony Gray signatory)
Avoma discovery call 12-Jun-2026 (Scanmarket expiry Dec-2027; next-FY project from May-2027)

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is clearly articulated and owned by Daniel Simmonds. On 12-Jun he detailed a fragmented, 'very clunky' stack (Halo intake, Scanmarket for S2C, Chrome River/Anydata for invoicing/analytics, Ligura for redlining, DocuSign, Adderant ERP) where 'a lot of systems, approvals tend to be done over email or DocuSign', with duplicate data entry, no orchestration, and buyers with 'no idea what's going on without speaking to us'. The process 'very much ends when the contract is signed' with 'no purchase order functionality at the moment'. On 26-Jun he reinforced frustrations: frequent new-supplier/one-off buying, manual reporting ('we have to chat with someone to get these reports'), and no proactive supplier-risk monitoring (a recent Iraq-based supplier gap). The pain owner is Daniel and his 5-person procurement team; the vision he stated is a single 'guided buyer experience' orchestrated platform.
sources: Avoma discovery call 12-Jun-2026 (fragmented stack, no PO, duplicate entry)
Avoma demo 26-Jun-2026 (new-supplier buying, manual reporting, supplier-risk gap)

champion.narrative: Daniel Simmonds (Head of Procurement) is a developing champion, not yet a committed one. What has developed: he proactively included Zycus in the RFI on the strength of prior awareness ('I know of Zycus... that's why we want to engage with you and include you in the RFI'), gave an unusually transparent discovery of the full systems landscape, budget structure and timeline, reacted strongly to the demo ('a million miles away from where we are', 'really, really good'), and is the process owner driving cadence. What is missing: he is running a neutral formal 6-7 vendor RFI rather than advocating for Zycus, has not introduced or opened a path to the COO/finance economic buyers, withheld the budget figure, and was explicit that the process beyond the RFI is 'up in the air'. He is engaged and friendly but currently protects Pinsent Masons' interests rather than coaching Zycus to win, so converting him into an advocate is the highest-leverage relationship move.

competition.narrative: This is a wide-open, early competitive field with no vendor preferred. Zycus is one of 6-7 vendors in a formal RFI ('6 or 7 on the list... then we'd probably shortlist that to 3, 4'), and Daniel did not name the peers. The real contest is against inertia: the incumbent Scanmarket (Unit4) source-to-contract renews by default at Dec-2027 if no decision is made, and any spend above the existing budget requires a business case that does not yet exist, so do-nothing/partial-scope is the strongest current threat. Ligura, an AI contract-redlining tool the firm has already signed for 12 months, owns the contract-AI use case and could carve the Zycus contracting module out of scope unless positioned as a coexistence/integration. Halo (ITSM) is the incumbent intake 'here to stay'. Zycus's wedge is a single orchestrated S2P platform with Merlin agentic intake inside Microsoft Teams, contract insta-review and autonomous negotiation, plus regulated-sector references, differentiated against a fragmented stack.
sources: Avoma demo 26-Jun-2026 (6-7 vendors, shortlist to 3-4, Ligura already signed)
Avoma discovery call 12-Jun-2026 (Scanmarket incumbent, Dec-2027 expiry, Halo intake, budget/business case)

deal_movement.summary: Steady forward motion in the RFI phase over the last four weeks: discovery, NDA, demo, RFI issuance and a live clarification exchange, with the buyer engaged as recently as 3-Jul. No slip. The gaps are structural (no EB, single-thread, undisclosed budget), not momentum.

items.change: Discovery call held with Daniel Simmonds; full systems landscape, £120M indirect spend and S2P vision mapped
Mutual NDA executed via DocuSign (Jamie James issued, Anthony Gray signatory)
45-minute art-of-the-possible demo delivered (Merlin intake, Anna autonomous negotiation, contract insta-review, spend analytics, supplier risk)
RFI issued to 6-7 vendors including Zycus; response deadline set to 27-Jul-2026
RFI clarification questions exchanged; Daniel returned answers, flagging several he 'can't answer at this stage'

competitive_position.summary: No vendor is preferred: Zycus is one of 6-7 in a formal RFI (peers unnamed by the buyer, so not invented here). The single strongest CURRENT threat is do-nothing / partial-scope, with incumbent Scanmarket (Unit4) source-to-contract renewing by default at Dec-2027 and above-budget spend gated behind an unwritten business case (Daniel, 12-Jun and 26-Jun). Ligura, an already-signed AI contract-redlining tool, is a live scope threat to the Zycus contracting module. Halo (ITSM intake) and the wider point stack (Chrome River, Anydata, DocuSign, Adderant) are incumbents to consolidate or integrate, not active rivals.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / status quo (fragmented-stack renewal)
competitors.quote: Whether we need additional budget and I can get approval for that... beyond the RFI it's very much up in the air (Daniel Simmonds, 12-Jun-2026).
competitors.how_we_win: Build the business case on £120M indirect spend, no-PO gaps and 40% wasted process time so a decision beats the Dec-2027 Scanmarket renewal default.

competitors.name: Scanmarket (Unit4)
competitors.quote: Scanmarket, or what was Scanmarket, now Unit4, as our source-to-contract solution... that's up December 2027, so that's the date we're aiming for (Daniel Simmonds, 12-Jun-2026).
competitors.how_we_win: Replace the fragmented S2C tooling with one orchestrated S2P data core before the Dec-2027 contract locks in another multi-year term.

competitors.name: Unnamed RFI peer vendors (6-7 in field)
competitors.quote: 6 or 7 on the list at the moment... then we'd probably shortlist that to 3, 4 and ask for some more detailed demos (Daniel Simmonds, 26-Jun-2026).
competitors.how_we_win: Differentiate on Merlin agentic intake inside Teams, contract insta-review and regulated-sector references to secure a top-3 shortlist slot.

competitors.name: Ligura
competitors.quote: We've made the decision to go with... we're trialling Ligura, and the contract lawyers in our team are trialling Ligura to do that redlining and review of contracts... is there a way we can still make sure it's part of the overall process? (Daniel Simmonds, 26-Jun-2026).
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus contract AI to coexist and integrate with Ligura (open-API) so the contracting module stays in scope via source-to-contract linkage.

competitors.name: Halo (ITSM intake)
competitors.quote: We made the decision to select Halo as our ITSM solution... that is here to stay. Whether we use that as intake is up for discussion (Daniel Simmonds, 12-Jun-2026).
competitors.how_we_win: Integrate Merlin guided intake with Halo rather than replace it; deliver procurement intake in Teams with zero change management.

competitors.name: Point stack: Chrome River / Anydata / DocuSign / Adderant
competitors.quote: Chrome River we've used since 2019... a separate spend analytics tool by Anydata... DocuSign firm-wide... payments via Adderant, our practice-management system (Daniel Simmonds, 12-Jun-2026).
competitors.how_we_win: Consolidate Anydata spend analytics (up for renewal next year) and invoicing onto one data core; coexist with DocuSign and integrate to Adderant via API.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong strategic fit on the vision (single orchestrated, AI-guided S2P platform) with two live exposures: catalog-light buying reduces the fit of catalog-driven flows, and the funded scope is narrower than the end-to-end ambition.

items.criterion: Single orchestrated, guided S2P platform replacing a fragmented stack
items.quote: Ideally where we want to get to is a guided buyer experience... a full cohesive, orchestrated platform (Daniel Simmonds).

items.criterion: PO functionality and downstream P2P (today absent)
items.quote: Our process very much ends when the contract is signed. We have no purchase order functionality at the moment (Daniel Simmonds).
We don't do a lot of catalog... we buy a lot of new stuff from a lot of new suppliers (Daniel Simmonds).

items.criterion: Contract AI coexisting with the already-signed Ligura
items.quote: Is there a way that we can still make sure it's part of the overall process? (Daniel Simmonds, on Ligura integration).

items.criterion: Adderant (practice-management ERP) integration for supplier/vendor data
items.quote: Adderant stores our transactional data and the vendor database... it will pull out via an API (buyer exchange, 26-Jun).

items.requirement: Submit the completed RFI response with indicative pricing by 27-Jul-2026
items.quote: RFI will be issued on the 28th June, response deadline will be the 27th July... we also would like some sort of indicative pricing at this stage.

items.deliverable: Share the Heineken autonomous-negotiation (Anna) one-time-purchase customer report offered to Daniel on the 26-Jun demo
items.grounding_quote: I can send you a report which we have with our customers. So Heineken is one of our customers... they have 32 business units.

items.quote: The COO is aware that we're looking at, but very much driven by us (Daniel, 12-Jun).
I won't share what our existing budget is with you, Claire (Daniel, 26-Jun).

items.area: Other 6 RFI vendors unnamed by the buyer
items.quote: 6 or 7 on the list at the moment (Daniel, 26-Jun); peers not named.

items.area: Contact-role data quality: Daniel Simmonds' OpportunityContactRole carries a Severn Trent email/title, but every activity and call confirms him as Head of Procurement at pinsentmasons.com
items.quote: daniel.simmonds@pinsentmasons.com... Head of Procurement (SF task email signature 3-Jul).
items.source: SF OpportunityContactRole vs SF Task emails

best_practice_check.summary: Early-stage RFI: the win levers are breaking single-thread to the budget owners, converting a neutral evaluator into an advocate, and closing the integration objections before shortlist.
flags: Break single-thread: Daniel is the only buyer contact; reach finance (owns P2P and budget) and the COO who sponsors the mandate before the shortlist.
Convert Daniel from neutral evaluator to champion: give him a business-case toolkit and an inside track so he advocates for Zycus in a 6-7 vendor RFI.
Win the integration story early: prove Adderant (practice-management ERP) data sync and Ligura coexistence in the RFI to avoid being dropped on technical grounds.

items.name: Daniel Simmonds
items.title: Head of Procurement
items.sentiment: Positive and highly engaged; transparent on landscape, budget structure and timeline; called the demo 'really, really good'. Not yet advocating for Zycus in a neutral formal RFI.
items.risk: Single point of contact; running a neutral 6-7 vendor evaluation and has not opened access to the economic buyer.
items.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (Evaluator) + SF Task emails daniel.simmonds@pinsentmasons.com + Avoma 12-Jun and 26-Jun-2026

items.title: Chief Operating Officer / finance lead (unnamed)
items.sentiment: Unknown; COO only 'aware', finance 'on board at early stage' but not engaged in evaluation.
items.risk: Not engaged. Any spend above the existing source-to-contract budget needs a business case to this unmapped approver; deal can stall at business-case stage.
items.source: Avoma discovery 12-Jun-2026 (Daniel: COO aware, finance owns P2P/budget)

items.name: Jamie James
items.title: Contracts / legal contact (NDA owner; title unconfirmed in Salesforce)
items.sentiment: Neutral; handled the NDA and DocuSign paper process.
items.risk: Procedural contact only; not a decision-maker.

items.title: Procurement data analyst (heard on call as 'Maggie/Samangy'; not in Salesforce)
items.sentiment: Positive; prior Zycus user at Babcock; interested in Adderant data integration and reporting automation.
items.risk: Transcript-only, unverified in Salesforce; end-user influence on adoption, not a buyer.
items.source: Avoma demo 26-Jun-2026 (transcript only, no Salesforce contact)

champion_strength.summary: Daniel Simmonds is a developing champion. What has developed: he proactively invited Zycus into the RFI on prior awareness, gave a fully transparent discovery of systems, budget structure and timeline, reacted strongly to the 26-Jun demo, and owns the process cadence. What is missing: he is running a neutral 6-7 vendor formal RFI rather than advocating, has not introduced or opened a path to the COO/finance economic buyers, withheld the budget figure, and called the path beyond the RFI 'up in the air'. Turning him into an advocate is the top relationship move post-RFI.
champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole (Evaluator) + Avoma discovery 12-Jun and demo 26-Jun-2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The agentic story landed well: Anthony Gray's single-platform 'control tower' narrative and Nishita Sareen's live Merlin intake, Anna autonomous negotiation and contract insta-review demos drew consistent positive reactions ('really, really good', 'looks good'). Positioning is strong; the follow-through is proving Merlin/contract AI against Pinsent's own requirements in the RFI and coexisting with Ligura.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field reads 7.0 / Moderate, and the calls corroborate genuine but not top-down AI appetite: Daniel engaged deeply with Merlin guided intake in Teams, autonomous negotiation and contract insta-review, and named 'a guided buyer experience' as the goal, but confirmed the initiative is procurement-driven ('the COO is aware... very much driven by us') rather than a CEO/board AI mandate. Appetite runs slightly above the Moderate label on the guided-intake and contract-AI use cases.

items.detail: No economic buyer engaged; mandate is procurement-driven with the COO only 'aware' and finance not in the evaluation.
Existing budget covers only source-to-contract and spend analytics; anything above needs an unwritten business case, and Daniel declined to disclose the figure.
Single-threaded on Daniel Simmonds; no finance, IT or C-level contact engaged.
Adderant (a practice-management ERP, not a standard ERP) sync and coexistence with the already-signed Ligura contract-AI tool are unproven and buyer-raised.
Catalog-light buying ('we don't do a lot of catalog') weakens catalog-driven flows and, with P2P owned by finance, risks narrowing the funded scope.

confidence_signals.summary: Live and engaged: two substantive buyer calls in three weeks, an NDA in place, and a 3-Jul clarification exchange. Not cooling. The limiters are structural (no EB, single-thread, undisclosed budget), not engagement.
items: Buyer returned RFI clarification answers 3-Jul-2026 ('Completed questions, any queries please let me know').
Daniel proactively included Zycus in the RFI and reacted strongly to the demo.
Process beyond the RFI is buyer-acknowledged as 'up in the air'; decision path not yet defined.

scope_change.from: Full Source-to-Pay ambition (S2C + eProcurement + eInvoicing + spend analytics + Merlin intake + iRisk + contract AI)
scope_change.to: Funded core of source-to-contract + spend analytics, with P2P / invoicing / eProcurement contingent on a finance-owned business case
scope_change.detail: Daniel confirmed existing budget covers only source-to-contract and spend analytics; P2P is 'very much finance's responsibility' and 'who knows' if part of the end solution, and catalog buying is a weak fit. A budget-defensive narrowing that lowers likely ACV and hands leverage to the do-nothing/renewal default.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is one of six to seven vendors in Pinsent Masons' formal S2P RFI with an engaged but neutral Head of Procurement (Daniel Simmonds) and a firm 27-Jul response deadline; the win hinges on turning Daniel from evaluator into advocate and reaching the finance/COO buyers who gate any spend above the existing source-to-contract budget.

win_position.text: Zycus is mid-pack but credible: Daniel Simmonds proactively invited Zycus into the RFI, gave a fully transparent discovery, and called the 26-Jun demo 'a million miles away from where we are', yet no vendor is preferred in a wide 6-7 vendor field (Avoma 12-Jun and 26-Jun).

No economic buyer is engaged: Daniel said the COO is only 'aware' and the initiative is 'very much driven by us', so any spend above the existing source-to-contract budget needs an unwritten business case (Avoma 12-Jun).

Single-threaded and budget-constrained: Daniel funds only source-to-contract and analytics, called P2P finance's decision and 'who knows' if in scope, and refused to disclose the budget (Avoma 26-Jun).

deal_momentum.text: Active RFI phase with a firm clock: RFI issued to the field 28-Jun and Zycus's response is due 27-Jul, with clarification questions exchanged as recently as 3-Jul (SF activity 3-Jul).
Commercials are entering: Daniel asked for indicative pricing in the RFI and named a Dec-2027 Scanmarket expiry and a next-financial-year project window from May-2027 (Avoma 26-Jun).
Momentum is still rep-driven and generic: the 26-Jun session was an 'art of the possible' demo, no senior buyer has joined, and the process beyond the RFI is 'up in the air' (Avoma 26-Jun).

customer_commitment.text: NDA signed via DocuSign on 16-Jun with Anthony Gray as signatory, and Daniel returned the RFI clarification answers on 3-Jul, showing genuine two-way engagement (SF activity 16-Jun and 3-Jul).
Commitment is procedural, not preferential: Daniel is running a neutral formal evaluation to shortlist to 3-4 vendors and has signalled no Zycus lean (Avoma 26-Jun).

deal_risk.text: Do-nothing / partial-scope is the primary threat: incumbent Scanmarket (Unit4) renews by default at Dec-2027 and above-budget spend is gated behind a business case that does not yet exist (Avoma 12-Jun).

Two integration objections are open: Adderant (a practice-management ERP, not a standard ERP) and the already-signed Ligura contract-AI tool, both raised by Daniel on 26-Jun, could carve modules out of scope (Avoma 26-Jun).

customer_commitment.evidence: Neutral formal RFI, no Zycus lean, budget undisclosed

deal_risk.evidence: No EB engaged; budget gated behind business case; do-nothing/Scanmarket renewal default; scope narrowing

items.action: In the RFI response, propose the Adderant API sync and Ligura coexistence approach to close both integration questions.
items.trigger: Daniel asked on 26-Jun whether Zycus can integrate with Ligura and pull vendor data from Adderant.
items.expected_effect: Removes the top technical objection before the shortlist and protects the contracting module scope.

items.action: Multi-thread into Pinsent Masons finance to confirm whether P2P and invoicing are in funded scope.
items.trigger: Daniel said finance owns P2P and any spend above the existing budget needs a business case.
items.expected_effect: Surfaces the economic buyer and protects ACV against a scope narrowing.

items.action: Ask Daniel to confirm the shortlist criteria and who joins the tailored demo.
items.trigger: Daniel said he will shortlist to 3-4 vendors for more detailed demos with the wider team.
items.expected_effect: Reveals the decision process and unlocks new stakeholders to multi-thread.

items.action: Offer a value session quantifying savings on the £120M indirect spend to seed the business case.
items.trigger: Daniel said above-budget spend requires a business case he cannot approve alone.
items.expected_effect: Arms the champion for executive approval and converts qualitative pain into an ROI case.

items.action: Line up a regulated-sector or law-firm reference to differentiate versus the other RFI vendors.
items.trigger: Daniel values Pinsent Masons as an innovative firm in a high-regulation environment.
items.expected_effect: Builds credibility and helps secure a top-3 shortlist slot.

customer_preference.evidence: Positive but not decisive: Daniel called the demo 'really, really good' and 'a million miles away from where we are', but is running a neutral 6-7 vendor RFI with no Zycus-specific next step beyond the shared process (Avoma 26-Jun).

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain and scale quantified (£120M indirect spend, 40% process time wasted, no PO functionality), but no customer-owned ROI or savings figure; an above-budget spend explicitly needs a business case (Avoma 12-Jun and 26-Jun).

evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: activities last 120d; field history to 2026-06-12 (record created)
